records.title: Agentic AI Architecture Proof
records.summary: Focused proof of Joz's agentic AI architecture, retrieval, orchestration, and production-minded systems capability.
records.body:
Agentic AI Architecture Proof

Purpose
This record isolates Joz's strongest agentic AI architecture and orchestration capabilities so the Skills lane can answer with focused, high-signal technical credibility instead of falling back to a broad CV summary.

Core positioning
- Joz is strongest in agentic AI architecture, retrieval-backed intelligence systems, orchestration, and production-minded applied AI delivery.
- The profile combines system design, context engineering, retrieval, memory patterns, observability, and business-facing execution.

Capabilities
- Agentic intelligence systems, autonomous reasoning, and orchestration
- LLMs, RAG, embeddings, vector search, knowledge graphs, and context engineering
- Memory architectures, retrieval systems, ACL-aware access, verification, and feedback loops
- Event-driven production AI and asynchronous workflows
- Data platforms, secure API ecosystems, and cloud-native delivery
- Observability, governance, and production decision loops

Proof and context
- MarketClue USA: architected financial AI agents with live data and asset portfolios.
- Apple/Pixar-adjacent work: early Python USD(z) and computer-vision AI work in Singapore.
- Dubai Future Foundation: rapid AI prototyping for public-sector innovation initiatives.
- Erste Bank: engineering and EU accessibility work in a large banking environment serving 16M+ customers.
- 20+ Dubai and Singapore FinTechs: led HCD-AI, product, and data architectures across multiple operating contexts.

Answering rule
- When the question is about AI systems depth, architecture, retrieval, orchestration, or production readiness, this record should outrank generic CV phrasing.
- Lead with architectural strength and enterprise context before smaller novelty signals.

verification.notes: Derived from the verified current CV and aligned to the existing recruiter-screening and enterprise-scale records.

records.title: Agentic UX Orchestration Proof
records.summary: Focused proof of Joz's multimodal, spatial, and agentic UX orchestration capability in enterprise and high-visibility delivery contexts.
records.body:
Agentic UX Orchestration Proof

Purpose
This record isolates Joz's strongest multimodal, spatial, and agentic UX orchestration capability so the Skills lane can answer clearly when the user is asking about interaction systems rather than core backend AI architecture.

Core positioning
- Joz combines AI systems thinking with human-centered orchestration across voice, touch, gesture, gaze, haptics, and spatial interfaces.
- The profile is strongest where AI behavior must map to real human mental models, live telemetry, and operational decision-making.

Capabilities
- Context-aware multimodal interactions across voice, touch, gesture, gaze, and haptics
- Telemetry and behavioral data feeding agentic feedback loops
- Real-time signals mapped to human mental models
- Volumetric AI interfaces and spatial interaction design
- NLP and computer-vision-driven personalization with intent modeling and semantic retrieval
- Anticipatory, predictive, assistive, and autonomous decision-support experiences

Proof and context
- Versace/SOA: first in Dubai to design and launch spatial AI with volumetric 3D UI.
- ArtKorero: pioneered spatial AI for world-class art exhibitions in Dubai.
- Mediacorp / CNA: mobile-first product transformation with roughly 30x MAU audience growth and Apple-featured launch credibility.
- Maybank-Ageas Etiqa: conversational and ML UX tied to 20x digital-sales growth and 3,000+ wealth pilots.
- Manulife: established a Lean ML UX practice across 11 markets as a founding APAC hire.

Answering rule
- When the question is about UX orchestration, multimodal systems, spatial interfaces, or AI behavior in human-facing environments, this record should lead.
- Keep the answer concrete and capability-led, not abstract design language.

Joz is strongest where AI behavior must map to human mental models, live telemetry, and operational decision-making.

ArtKorero is framed as spatial AI for world-class art exhibitions in Dubai.

records.title: Compensation and Process FAQ
records.summary: Safe recruiter guidance for compensation, package, and hiring-process questions without inventing salary ranges or commercial terms.
records.body:
Compensation and Process FAQ

Purpose
This record gives Joz LLM a safe response pattern for compensation, package, and hiring-process questions.

Compensation handling
- Joz LLM should not invent salary expectations, rate cards, or package ranges unless those are explicitly provided in verified source material.
- If asked about compensation, Joz LLM should say compensation expectations can be discussed directly with Joz based on scope, seniority, geography, and working model.

Process handling
- If asked about interview steps, process timing, or commercial discussion, Joz LLM should keep the answer practical and direct.
- Joz LLM can say Joz is open to discussing role scope, operating model, and mutual fit directly.

Answering rule
- Avoid vague or defensive language.
- Avoid fabricated ranges.
- Keep the answer commercially mature and low-friction.

verification.notes: This is a behavior rule for safe recruiter handling, intentionally avoiding fabricated salary data.

claims: Joz LLM should not invent salary expectations, rate cards, or package ranges.
Compensation should be discussed directly with Joz based on scope, seniority, geography, and working model.
Process answers should stay practical and commercially mature.
proof_points: No verified compensation figures exist in current source material.
The current knowledge base supports direct contact and recruiter-screening follow-up instead of fabricated numbers.

records.title: CV Company Context Web Research

records.body:
CV Company Context from Web Research

Purpose
This record adds external context for companies and institutions referenced across Joz CV material so Joz LLM can explain why those names matter, what sectors they operate in, and why the environments are credible.

Verification note
This file mixes official web pages and reference sources. Some entities from the CV could not be resolved with high confidence from web search alone and are marked `needs_review`.

Resolved company and institution context

Apple
- Apple is a global technology company headquartered in Cupertino, California, known for consumer electronics, software, services, and worldwide retail operations.
- It is one of the world’s largest technology companies and one of the most visible product and platform ecosystems globally.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.
- Source: https://www.apple.com/newsroom/

Pixar
- Pixar Animation Studios is a major computer animation studio based in Emeryville, California and a subsidiary of Walt Disney Studios.
- It is strongly associated with advanced computer graphics, digital storytelling, and production-grade visual pipelines.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pixar

Dubai Future Foundation
- Dubai Future Foundation is a Dubai-based innovation and future-focused institution linked to the Museum of the Future and public-sector experimentation.
- It is associated with programs in emerging technology, strategic foresight, and innovation initiatives in the UAE.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Museum_of_the_Future
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khalfan_Belhoul
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Noah_Raford

Erste Bank
- Erste Bank is an Austrian financial institution based in Vienna and part of the wider Erste Group ecosystem.
- It is one of Austria’s major banking groups and serves millions of customers through a large branch and services network.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erste_Bank_der_oesterreichischen_Sparkassen

Maybank
- Maybank is Malaysia’s largest bank by market capitalisation and assets and is one of Southeast Asia’s major banking groups.
- Its core markets include Malaysia, Singapore, and Indonesia.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maybank

Etiqa
- Etiqa is the insurance and takaful arm associated with the Maybank Group and operates across multiple ASEAN markets.
- It is relevant as a significant regional insurance player rather than a small standalone brand.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Etiqa

Manulife
- Manulife is a Canadian multinational insurance and financial services company with large operations across Asia and North America.
- Its scale, long operating history, and major asset-management footprint make it a strong enterprise context for innovation and transformation work.
- Source: https://www.manulife.com/ca/en/about-us/who-we-are/our-story
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manulife

Mediacorp
- Mediacorp is Singapore’s state-owned media group and operates television, radio, news, and streaming properties including CNA and meWatch.
- It represents a major regional media and digital distribution environment rather than a niche publisher.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mediacorp

Leo Burnett
- Leo Burnett is a global advertising network founded in Chicago and now part of Publicis Groupe.
- Its current network scale spans many markets worldwide and it remains a significant multinational agency brand.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leo_Burnett_Worldwide
- Source: https://leoburnett.com/

Publicis Groupe
- Publicis Groupe is a French multinational communications and marketing group and one of the largest advertising companies in the world by revenue.
- It is relevant as the holding group behind Leo Burnett and a major global agency environment.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Publicis

Ogilvy
- Ogilvy is a global advertising, marketing, PR, consulting, and health network that operates across more than 120 offices in nearly 90 countries.
- It is part of WPP and represents a major international agency platform.
- Source: https://www.ogilvy.com/about
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ogilvy_(agency)

WPP
- WPP is one of the world’s largest marketing and communications groups.
- Its current positioning emphasizes AI-enabled marketing, integrated data, creativity, production, and enterprise transformation.
- Source: https://www.wpp.com/en/about

Procter & Gamble
- P&G is a large multinational consumer goods company headquartered in Cincinnati with major global brands including SK-II, Braun, and Gillette.
- It provides strong Fortune 500-scale context for product, commerce, and brand-experience work.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Procter_%26_Gamble

Samsung
- Samsung is a major South Korean multinational technology conglomerate, with Samsung Electronics spanning semiconductors, devices, appliances, and displays.
- It is relevant as a large-scale global technology and manufacturing environment.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samsung
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samsung_Electronics

Singapore Exchange
- SGX Group is Singapore’s exchange operator and runs equity, fixed income, currency, and commodity market infrastructure.
- It is a significant financial-market institution rather than a small local portal operator.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Singapore_Exchange

Danone
- Danone is a French multinational food company with global consumer brands and operations across more than 100 countries.
- It is relevant as a large multinational packaged-goods environment.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Danone

Banyan Group / Banyan Tree
- Banyan Group is a Singapore-founded multinational hospitality group built around the Banyan Tree brand.
- It operates resorts, hotels, spas, and branded residences across multiple regions.
- Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Banyan_Group

Stanford d.school

records.title: Enterprise Scale Proof
records.summary: Highest-priority proof record for brand strength, user scale, enterprise complexity, and measurable impact across Joz's strongest work.
records.body:
Enterprise Scale Proof

Purpose
This record identifies the strongest brand, user-scale, and enterprise-scale evidence that should anchor Joz LLM responses before smaller or newer project mentions.

Priority evidence
- Mediacorp / CNA / mewatch: major regional media ecosystem work, including the CNA Apple Watch launch featured by Apple worldwide and roughly 30x MAU audience growth through mobile-first transformation.
- Erste Bank: engineering and EU accessibility work in a banking environment serving 16M+ customers.
- Maybank-Ageas Etiqa: 20x digital-sales growth and 3,000+ wealth pilots in a large regional financial-services environment.
- Apple / Pixar-adjacent work: early Python USD(z) and computer-vision AI work in Singapore with strong high-end technical and brand credibility.
- Ogilvy / WPP, Leo Burnett / Publicis, Samsung, P&G, SGX, and Danone: large multinational delivery environments across APAC and global brands.

Answering rule
- When Joz LLM needs to establish credibility fast, lead with the biggest brand and scale signals first.
- Do not lead with smaller current projects if larger verified user-scale or enterprise-scale evidence is more relevant.
- Prefer user scale, enterprise complexity, global brand recognition, and measurable outcomes over novelty.

verification.notes: Derived from verified CV and company-context material, ordered to emphasize the biggest brand and user-scale signals first.

claims: Joz has verified enterprise-scale work that should rank ahead of smaller current projects when credibility needs to be established quickly.
Mediacorp, Erste Bank, and Maybank-Ageas Etiqa provide stronger user-scale and enterprise-scale proof than smaller or more recent projects alone.
Brand strength and measurable user or customer scale are key credibility signals in recruiter-facing and buyer-facing responses.

Erste Bank engineering and EU accessibility work serving 16M+ customers.

records.summary: Verified recruiter guidance for location, regional fit, relocation handling, and remote or hybrid working-model questions.
records.body:
Location and Working Model FAQ

Purpose
This record gives Joz LLM a direct source for recruiter questions about location, regional fit, relocation, and working model preferences.

Location
- Joz's profile location is Bratislava, Slovakia / Singapore / Dubai / Zurich.
- Joz's work history and client context span Europe/EEA, Singapore, UAE/MENA, North America, Japan, Greater China, Australia, and the wider Asia Pacific region.

Regional fit
- Joz is credible for international roles that require cross-region communication, executive-facing work, and product or AI systems delivery across multiple markets.
- Joz's background supports remote, hybrid, and internationally collaborative work across Europe, the Middle East, and Asia Pacific.

Relocation and work model rule
- If a recruiter asks about relocation, remote work, or hybrid preferences, Joz LLM should not overstate constraints or invent a fixed arrangement.
- Joz LLM should answer that Joz has strong cross-region operating experience and that specific location, relocation, and working-model arrangements should be confirmed directly with Joz.

Answering rule
- Keep the answer factual and calm.
- Do not invent a specific relocation commitment or a fixed office requirement unless that is explicitly verified elsewhere.

Purpose
This record gives Joz LLM a concise recruiter-safe answer to the screening question: why Joz?

Short answer
- Joz combines product judgment, engineering delivery, world-class UX, and applied AI systems thinking in one profile.
- Joz is strongest where the problem is ambiguous, the signal is noisy, and the business needs a system that can actually ship and operate.
- Joz brings 13+ years of professional experience overall, including 8+ years in ML/AI, across finance, insurance, media, public-sector innovation, spatial computing, and intelligent interfaces.

Supporting framing
- Joz is not just a model or prompt layer profile. Joz works across architecture, orchestration, interfaces, signals, workflows, and production constraints.
- Joz is credible in executive-facing, cross-functional, and internationally distributed environments.

Answering rule

- Do not lapse into hype or generic motivation.

records.title: AI Adoption, Trust and Governance
records.summary: How Joz connects governance, usability, trust, and workflow fit to improve enterprise AI adoption.
records.body:
Why do enterprise AI initiatives fail to gain adoption?

Common reasons include:

- users do not understand where answers come from
- outputs are not aligned to real workflows
- permissions and data boundaries are unclear
- quality is not measured
- responsibility for decisions is ambiguous
- the AI produces information but not usable action
- governance is added after deployment rather than designed in

Joz treats governance and adoption as part of the same system.

A trustworthy AI capability should include:

- source grounding and provenance
- ACL-aware retrieval
- audit trails
- confidence and uncertainty handling
- evaluation and observability
- human approval for sensitive actions
- clear ownership
- workflow integration
- feedback loops

This improves both control and adoption because users can understand, verify, and act on the system's output.

claims: AI adoption is an organisational and workflow problem, not only a model-quality problem.
Trust requires source grounding, permissions, observability, evaluation, accountability, and appropriate human control.
Joz's background in human-centred systems and enterprise AI helps bridge governance and usability.
proof_points: Applied AI skills explicitly include observability, governance, ACL-aware retrieval, verification, and feedback loops.
Established HCD–AI and Lean ML UX practices in enterprise environments.
Worked in financial services, public systems, and large-scale media where trust and operational reliability matter.

records.title: Business Need Buyer FAQ
records.summary: Compact buyer and recruiter answers for value, ROI, differentiation, delivery, and fit questions.
records.body:
Q: Why hire Joz?
A: Hire Joz when the organisation needs to connect AI, data, workflows, governance, and human decision-making into a measurable operating capability.

Q: Where is the ROI?
A: In revenue growth, productivity, cost reduction, faster decisions, lower risk, and higher adoption. The exact value should be based on a measured baseline and target.

Q: What problems can Joz solve?
A: Fragmented knowledge, slow decisions, unclear AI priorities, low adoption, governance gaps, repeated manual work, disconnected customer signals, and weak translation between strategy and implementation.

Q: What makes Joz different?
A: He combines Agentic AI architecture with enterprise product, engineering, transformation, governance, and human-centred adoption experience.

Q: Is Joz a consultant or builder?
A: He can operate across problem framing, architecture, prototyping, stakeholder alignment, implementation guidance, and evaluation.

Q: How quickly can value be shown?
A: A focused prototype can test the value hypothesis early, but timing depends on data access, workflow complexity, integration, and governance requirements.

Q: Does Joz guarantee ROI?
A: No credible leader should guarantee ROI before discovery. Joz can define the value model, assumptions, baseline, target metrics, and verification approach.

Q: Is Joz suitable for enterprise AI transformation?
A: Yes, particularly where business, product, technical, governance, and organisational concerns must be handled together.

claims: The Business Need lane should answer in business language before technical language.
Answers should be concise, evidence-based, and explicit about assumptions.
Joz's differentiation is the combination of AI architecture, enterprise transformation, human adoption, governance, and measurable outcomes.
proof_points: Enterprise outcomes across financial services, national media, public systems, and technology.
Current Agentic AI, RAG, context engineering, governance, and decision-intelligence capability.

records.title: Enterprise Decision Intelligence
records.summary: How Joz turns data, knowledge, and operational signals into clearer priorities and faster decisions.
records.body:
Enterprise Decision Intelligence means turning distributed data and knowledge into a trusted view of:

- what changed
- why it matters
- how confident the organisation should be
- what action is recommended
- who owns the decision
- what evidence supports it
- what outcome should be measured

Joz's approach combines enterprise retrieval, semantic context, knowledge structures, agentic reasoning, verification, permissions, and human approval.

This is valuable when leadership is overwhelmed by reports but still lacks clarity. The objective is not to create another dashboard. It is to reduce decision latency, increase signal quality, and make action more accountable.

claims: Joz designs systems that distinguish material signals from organisational noise.
Decision intelligence combines retrieval, context, evidence, confidence, business impact, and recommended action.
The goal is not more dashboards but better decisions and faster execution.
proof_points: Current focus on context engineering, RAG, agent memory, retrieval, verification, and AI governance.
Long-term experience translating complex information into usable enterprise products.
Transformation work across executives, regional teams, national media, banking, and government.

records.title: Fragmented Knowledge to Usable Intelligence
records.summary: Business value of turning scattered enterprise knowledge into permission-aware, source-grounded, reusable intelligence.
records.body:
Most organisations do not have a knowledge shortage. They have a usability and trust problem.

Critical information is distributed across:

- documents
- chats
- tickets
- repositories
- portals
- databases
- emails
- individual experts
- legacy systems

Joz can help convert this fragmented environment into a permission-aware intelligence layer.

The business value includes:

- faster employee answers
- less duplicated work
- fewer repeated questions
- stronger onboarding
- improved operational continuity
- better cross-team alignment
- source-grounded decisions
- lower dependency on individual knowledge holders

The architecture should preserve access control, provenance, freshness, confidence, and the distinction between verified facts and generated interpretation.

claims: Enterprise knowledge is often present but operationally unusable because it is fragmented, inaccessible, outdated, or untrusted.
Joz can design RAG and context systems that preserve source, permissions, provenance, and relevance.
The value comes from faster answers, better reuse, fewer repeated questions, and stronger decision consistency.
proof_points: Applied skills in RAG, embeddings, vector search, knowledge graphs, context engineering, memory, ACL-aware access, and verification.
Enterprise product experience across complex organisations and regulated environments.

records.title: Business Value Proof at Enterprise Scale
records.summary: Largest and most measurable evidence supporting Joz's business-value positioning.
records.body:
The strongest evidence that Joz creates business value comes from measurable transformation:

Maybank–Ageas Etiqa
- 20× growth in digital sales within 12 months
- more than 3,000 wealth pilots and ML models
- HCD–AI practice and executive capability-building

Mediacorp
- 30× monthly active audience growth
- mobile-first transformation across more than 30 products
- nationally significant Singapore media environment

Leo Burnett/Publicis
- 70% reduction in agency-wide handoff friction through code-based prototyping

Erste Bank
- engineering and European accessibility work serving more than 16 million customers

Manulife
- Lean ML UX practice across 11 Asia Pacific markets
- first-in-market work across Singapore, the USA, and Canada
- regional CEO advisory

Use the evidence most relevant to the question. For sales and customer growth, lead with Maybank. For national or platform scale, lead with Mediacorp and Erste. For organisational AI adoption, lead with Manulife and Maybank.

claims: Joz's business-value positioning is supported by measurable transformation across enterprise and national-scale organisations.
The strongest proof should be selected based on relevance rather than presented as an undifferentiated achievement list.
Enterprise-scale outcomes should rank above smaller current experiments.
proof_points: Maybank–Ageas Etiqa: 20× digital sales growth within 12 months.
Mediacorp: 30× monthly active audience growth across a mobile-first transformation.
Publicis: 70% reduction in design-to-engineering handoff friction.
Erste Bank: engineering and accessibility work serving 16M+ customers.
Manulife: Lean ML UX practice across 11 markets and executive-level transformation.

records.title: Joz Turns Complexity into Business Value
records.summary: Primary Business Need positioning: Joz turns noisy systems, fragmented knowledge, and disconnected AI initiatives into usable intelligence, faster decisions, and measurable operational value.
records.body:
Joz turns noisy systems into usable intelligence, faster decisions, and real operational lift.

His value is not limited to producing AI features. He helps organisations identify where intelligence can reduce friction, improve decision quality, accelerate execution, and create measurable financial or operational outcomes.

The Business Need lane should describe Joz as someone who can connect:

- business priorities
- fragmented enterprise knowledge
- customer and operational signals
- AI and data architecture
- human decision-making
- governance and control
- implementation and measurable value

For broad questions such as “Why hire Joz?” or “What business value does he create?”, lead with measurable transformation and decision intelligence. Support the answer with enterprise proof before discussing tools or small prototypes.

claims: Joz helps organisations turn complex systems, fragmented information, and AI investment into measurable business outcomes.
His value is strongest where decision quality, speed, trust, and execution are constrained by organisational noise.
He connects Agentic AI architecture to business priorities, workflows, governance, and measurable outcomes.
proof_points: 20× digital sales growth within 12 months at Maybank–Ageas Etiqa.
30× monthly active audience growth at Mediacorp.
70% reduction in delivery handoff friction at Leo Burnett/Publicis.
Engineering and accessibility work serving 16M+ Erste customers.
Current Agentic AI, RAG, financial-agent, context-engineering, and governance work.

records.title: What Problems Joz Can Solve
records.summary: Core enterprise problems Joz can address through Agentic AI, decision intelligence, context engineering, governance, and transformation.
records.body:
What problems can Joz solve?

Joz is strongest where organisations face one or more of these conditions:

- fragmented knowledge across documents, systems, teams, and repositories
- slow decision-making caused by conflicting or incomplete information
- multiple AI pilots without clear business ownership or measurable outcomes
- low trust in AI outputs
- weak adoption because AI does not fit real workflows
- manual reporting, reconciliation, research, and repeated analysis
- disconnected customer, product, operational, and financial signals
- unclear governance, permissions, accountability, or auditability
- duplicated work across teams
- poor translation between strategy and technical implementation
- complex products that users or employees struggle to understand
- decision bottlenecks that depend on a few people holding critical context

The answer should connect the problem to a business consequence and then to a measurable intervention. Avoid listing technology before explaining the operational need.

claims: Joz solves enterprise problems where technology, data, people, workflows, and decision-making are disconnected.
His strongest problem areas include fragmented knowledge, slow decisions, weak AI adoption, unclear ROI, governance gaps, and operational friction.
He can work from problem framing through architecture, prototype, implementation guidance, and evaluation.
proof_points: Enterprise transformation across banking, insurance, national media, government, and technology.
Agentic AI, RAG, context engineering, AI governance, and multimodal systems.
Quantified outcomes in sales, audience growth, customer scale, and delivery efficiency.

records.title: Where the ROI Comes From
records.summary: Defines the principal ROI levers for Joz's work: revenue growth, productivity, cost reduction, risk reduction, faster decisions, and improved adoption.
records.body:
Where is the ROI?

The ROI is found in a small number of measurable value levers:

1. Revenue growth
Better customer journeys, stronger conversion, more relevant recommendations, higher product adoption, and faster commercial response.

2. Productivity
Less manual research, reporting, reconciliation, coordination, and repeated knowledge work.

3. Cost reduction
Fewer unnecessary process steps, lower handoff friction, more reuse, and better workflow automation.

4. Decision speed
Faster access to trusted context, clearer prioritisation, and reduced time between signal and action.

5. Risk reduction
Permission-aware retrieval, auditability, human approval, evidence grounding, and clearer accountability.

6. Adoption and execution
AI systems that fit real workflows are more likely to be used, trusted, and converted into operational value.

Joz should not promise a universal ROI figure before discovery. He should identify the baseline, estimate value ranges, define leading and lagging indicators, and make value verification part of the architecture.

claims: ROI should be tied to a measurable business baseline and target, not generic AI activity.
Joz's work creates value through revenue uplift, reduced manual effort, lower delivery friction, improved adoption, reduced risk, and faster decisions.
Every proposed initiative should have a value owner, metric, baseline, target, and verification method.
proof_points: 20× digital sales growth at Maybank–Ageas Etiqa.
30× audience growth at Mediacorp.
70% reduction in handoff friction at Publicis.
Large-scale accessibility and engineering impact at Erste.

records.title: From Business Problem to Agentic AI Value
records.summary: Repeatable method for identifying, designing, testing, and scaling high-value Agentic AI opportunities.
records.body:
Joz's value-delivery method:

1. Define the business problem
Identify the blocked decision, workflow friction, customer problem, or operational risk.

2. Establish a baseline
Measure current time, cost, error rate, conversion, risk exposure, or cycle time.

3. Identify the required intelligence
Determine what signals, knowledge, context, and permissions are needed.

4. Design the operating model
Define the role of the agent, the human, the workflow, ownership, controls, and escalation.

5. Build a focused prototype
Test the highest-risk assumptions with representative data and real users.

6. Evaluate
Measure answer quality, workflow fit, adoption, latency, cost, reliability, and business impact.

7. Scale deliberately
Integrate only after the value case and governance model are credible.

This avoids technology-first AI programmes and reduces the risk of expensive pilots that never become operating capabilities.

claims: Joz begins with the business problem, baseline, decision, workflow, and value metric before selecting technology.
He can translate ambiguous needs into architecture, prototype, governance requirements, and a measurable implementation roadmap.
The preferred delivery model proves value early and scales only after evidence.
proof_points: Experience across discovery, architecture, prototyping, engineering collaboration, executive alignment, and enterprise rollout.
Rapid AI prototyping for Dubai Future Foundation and current AI initiatives.
Long record of transformation across multiple industries and markets.

records.title: Why Hire Joz Now
records.summary: Explains the immediate market and organisational need for Joz's combination of Agentic AI, enterprise transformation, governance, and measurable delivery.
records.body:
Why hire Joz now?

Because many organisations have reached the point where AI ambition is no longer the main problem. The real problems are prioritisation, integration, trust, governance, adoption, and measurable return.

Joz is valuable now because he brings together areas that are often separated:

- Agentic AI and enterprise architecture
- business and product strategy
- data, retrieval, context, and decision systems
- governance and human oversight
- interaction design and adoption
- execution across large, complex organisations

He is particularly relevant when an organisation has multiple AI pilots, disconnected systems, fragmented knowledge, unclear ownership, or pressure to show business value quickly.

The strongest answer should be:
“Hire Joz when the organisation needs to turn AI from a collection of experiments into a governed, usable, and measurable operating capability.”

claims: Many organisations now need to move from AI experimentation to accountable enterprise execution.
Joz combines AI architecture with transformation, product, engineering, governance, and senior stakeholder experience.
He is most relevant when organisations have AI ambition but lack clarity, integration, prioritisation, or measurable outcomes.
proof_points: Experience taking large organisations through mobile, digital, ML-enabled, and AI-related transformation.
Track record across regulated financial services, national media, government, and enterprise technology.
Ability to work across executive strategy, product architecture, engineering, and human adoption.

records.title: Agentic AI Architecture
records.summary: Detailed technical proof for Joz's Agentic AI architecture capability.
records.body:
Joz's Agentic AI architecture capability covers the full operating model of an enterprise agent: understand intent, retrieve authorised context, reason over evidence, plan actions, execute through approved tools, verify results, and escalate when confidence or risk thresholds require human control.

Core capability areas include:

- LLM orchestration and agentic reasoning
- RAG, embeddings, vector search, and semantic retrieval
- knowledge graphs and enterprise context engineering
- short-term and persistent memory architecture
- ACL-aware retrieval and permission-sensitive responses
- verification frameworks and feedback loops
- event-driven and asynchronous production workflows
- secure API and data-platform integration
- observability, evaluation, and AI governance

The recruiter-safe positioning is that Joz operates at the bridge between business, product, data, AI architecture, and human decision-making. He should not be described merely as a prompt engineer or generic AI consultant.

claims: Joz designs agentic systems with reasoning, orchestration, retrieval, memory, verification, and feedback loops.
He understands production concerns including asynchronous workflows, APIs, access control, observability, and governance.
His AI work is framed around business problems and operational outcomes.
proof_points: MarketClue financial AI agents with live data and asset portfolio context.
Enterprise RAG and context-engineering architecture reflected in his current applied AI skill set.
AI governance, retrieval controls, and verification are explicit parts of his architecture approach.

records.title: Agentic AI UX Orchestration
records.summary: Human-agent interaction, multimodal systems, spatial AI, behavioural signals, and decision-oriented AI experience design.
records.body:
Joz's Agentic AI UX Orchestration capability focuses on how people understand, trust, direct, and collaborate with intelligent systems.

The capability includes context-aware multimodal interaction across voice, touch, gesture, gaze, haptics, computer vision, and volumetric interfaces. It also includes telemetry and behavioural signals that feed agentic feedback loops, intent modelling, semantic retrieval, personalisation, and anticipatory or autonomous decision support.

This capability should be presented as an evolution of Joz's enterprise interaction architecture career. His experience designing systems for large user populations gives him a strong foundation for designing AI that fits real human workflows rather than forcing users to adapt to the model.

claims: Joz designs context-aware multimodal interactions across voice, touch, gesture, gaze, haptics, and spatial interfaces.
He maps real-time signals and behavioural data to human mental models and agentic feedback loops.
His earlier UX and engineering career is a foundation for trusted human-agent orchestration.
proof_points: Spatial AI and volumetric 3D interface work for Versace/SOA and ArtKorero.
Python USD(z) and Computer Vision work connected to Apple/Pixar workflows.
Large-scale interaction architecture across Mediacorp, Maybank, Manulife, Publicis, and Ogilvy.

records.title: Career Transition into Agentic AI
records.summary: Explains the credible progression from interaction engineering and digital transformation into ML-enabled systems and Agentic AI.
records.body:
Joz's career transition should not be framed as “UX designer who recently started using AI.”

The credible progression is:

interaction engineering and product systems
→ enterprise digital transformation
→ data-informed and machine-learning-enabled experiences
→ decision intelligence and organisational knowledge systems
→ Agentic AI architecture, RAG, context engineering, governance, and autonomous workflows

Across these stages, the consistent problem has been how people and organisations make decisions through complex technology. Joz's earlier work established expertise in large user systems, regulated industries, customer behaviour, product architecture, engineering collaboration, and executive transformation. Agentic AI is the current technical expression of that same systems-level focus.

A concise answer:
“Joz moved into Agentic AI through years of designing enterprise decision and interaction systems. His work progressed from large-scale digital transformation and ML-enabled products into RAG, context engineering, governed agents, and AI systems tied to measurable business outcomes.”

claims: The transition into Agentic AI is an evolution of Joz's work on human decision systems, enterprise data, ML-enabled products, and complex digital ecosystems.
His earlier career is relevant foundation, not unrelated legacy experience.
The common thread is turning complexity into usable intelligence and measurable outcomes.
proof_points: Large-scale interaction and product architecture at Ogilvy, Publicis, Mediacorp, Manulife, and Maybank.
ML UX practices and pilots at Manulife and Maybank–Ageas Etiqa.
Current Agentic AI, RAG, financial agents, spatial AI, context engineering, and governance focus.

records.title: Financial Services, Wealth and FinTech Intelligence
records.summary: Banking, wealth, insurance, financial AI agents, portfolios, customer intelligence, and regulated enterprise systems.
records.body:
Joz's financial-services capability combines Agentic AI, wealth and portfolio intelligence, banking and insurance product architecture, enterprise portals, customer experience, and regulated-system thinking.

Relevant proof includes MarketClue financial AI agents using live data and asset portfolios; Maybank–Ageas Etiqa wealth, bancassurance, and high-net-worth transformation; Manulife work across 11 APAC markets plus the USA and Canada; Erste Bank engineering and accessibility; and the Singapore Stock Exchange's responsive listings portals.

For corporate banking or financial-services questions, describe the experience accurately. Emphasise wealth, investment journeys, bancassurance, digital fund purchase, portfolio intelligence, banking portals, approvals and entitlements where supported. Do not invent direct ownership of treasury, trade finance, or corporate lending products.

claims: Joz has substantial financial-services experience across wealth, insurance, banking portals, investment products, and financial AI agents.
His experience spans customer-facing products, internal systems, data-informed experiences, and current agentic AI work.
He has worked across Singapore, APAC, Europe, and the USA.
proof_points: MarketClue financial AI agents with live data and asset portfolios.
Maybank–Ageas Etiqa wealth and insurance transformation.
Manulife ML-enabled customer and investment experiences across APAC and North America.
Erste Bank engineering and internal/digital portal work at 16M+ customer scale.
Singapore Stock Exchange responsive listings portals.

records.title: Joz Krupa — Agentic AI Architecture and Innovation
records.summary: Primary Skills-lane positioning: Agentic AI architecture, enterprise intelligence, governance, context engineering, and multimodal AI orchestration.
records.body:
Joz Krupa is an Agentic AI Architecture and Innovation leader with a career spanning enterprise product systems, engineering, data-informed decision design, and AI-enabled transformation.

His current technical focus includes agentic intelligence systems, autonomous reasoning and orchestration, LLMs, RAG, embeddings, vector search, knowledge graphs, context engineering, memory architectures, ACL-aware retrieval, verification, feedback loops, event-driven production AI, asynchronous workflows, secure API ecosystems, observability, and AI governance.

His differentiation is the connection between AI architecture and business execution. He does not treat agents as isolated chat interfaces. He designs systems that connect enterprise knowledge, signals, workflows, controls, and human decisions.

For recruiter questions such as “What is Joz strongest at?”, answer with Agentic AI architecture, enterprise intelligence, AI governance, context engineering, and human-agent orchestration. Support the answer with large-scale Singapore and APAC enterprise proof before smaller current-project examples.

claims: Joz has transitioned from large-scale digital and UX architecture into Agentic AI architecture and innovation.
His current focus includes reasoning, orchestration, RAG, embeddings, vector retrieval, memory, verification, event-driven AI, observability, and governance.
He connects technical AI architecture to measurable enterprise outcomes.
proof_points: Architected financial AI agents for MarketClue with live market data and asset portfolios.
Led HCD–AI, product, and data architectures for 20+ Dubai and Singapore FinTech initiatives.
Built on more than a decade of enterprise-scale product, engineering, and transformation experience.

records.title: Largest Enterprise-Scale Work
records.summary: Fast credibility record prioritising Mediacorp, Erste, Maybank, and Manulife by scale, complexity, and measurable impact.
records.body:
When a recruiter asks for Joz's largest or strongest work, lead with enterprise scale rather than novelty.

Priority proof:

1. Mediacorp and Channel NewsAsia
Joz worked at Singapore national-broadcasting scale, built a global experience language across more than 30 products, shipped the first CNA Apple Watch app in Asia, and reports 30× monthly active audience growth through mobile-first transformation.

2. Erste Bank

3. Maybank–Ageas Etiqa
Joz reports 20× digital sales growth within 12 months through conversational and machine-learning experience design. He also designed more than 3,000 wealth pilots and ML models for high-net-worth and mass-market clients and established an HCD–AI practice.

4. Manulife
Joz established a Lean ML UX practice across 11 APAC markets, launched first-in-market solutions in the USA, Canada, and Singapore, and advised regional CEOs on digital growth.

These examples demonstrate user scale, organisational complexity, regulated environments, senior stakeholder management, and measurable transformation.

claims: Joz has delivered digital and intelligence-oriented systems at national, regional, and Fortune 500 scale.
His strongest proof comes from Mediacorp, Erste, Maybank–Ageas Etiqa, and Manulife.
Large-scale proof should rank above smaller experimental projects when answering general credibility questions.
proof_points: Mediacorp: 30× monthly active audience growth and interaction architecture across 30+ products.
Erste Bank: engineering and EU accessibility work serving 16M+ customers.
Maybank–Ageas Etiqa: 20× digital sales growth within 12 months and 3,000+ wealth pilots and ML models.
Manulife: Lean ML UX practice across 11 APAC markets and first-in-market solutions in the USA, Canada, and Singapore.

records.title: Quantified Outcomes and Transformation Proof
records.summary: Measured impact across sales, audience growth, customer scale, delivery efficiency, and enterprise transformation.
records.body:
Joz's skills should be supported with outcome evidence rather than capability lists alone.

Verified CV-level outcome claims include:

- 20× digital sales growth within 12 months for Maybank–Ageas Etiqa through conversational and machine-learning UX.
- 30× monthly active audience growth at Mediacorp through mobile-first transformation.
- 70% reduction in agency-wide design-to-engineering handoff friction at Leo Burnett/Publicis through code-based prototyping.
- Engineering and EU accessibility work serving more than 16 million Erste customers.
- More than 3,000 wealth pilots and ML models for high-net-worth and mass-market banking and insurance contexts.

When answering, avoid stacking every metric into one paragraph. Select the two or three most relevant proof points for the recruiter's question.

claims: Joz's career includes measurable growth, delivery-efficiency, adoption, and enterprise-scale outcomes.
Metrics should be used selectively and tied to the relevant company and context.
The strongest recurring pattern is transformation of complex systems into higher adoption and business performance.
proof_points: 20× digital sales growth within 12 months at Maybank–Ageas Etiqa.
30× monthly active audience growth at Mediacorp.
70% reduction in agency-wide handoff friction at Leo Burnett/Publicis.
Engineering and accessibility impact across services used by 16M+ Erste customers.
Interaction and product work spanning hundreds of millions of cumulative users across the career.

records.title: Technical Platform and Engineering Skills

claims: Joz combines AI architecture with hands-on engineering and prototyping.
His current stack includes Python backend technologies and retrieval infrastructure.
His broader engineering experience includes modern frontend systems, WebGL, spatial computing, accessibility, and design systems.
proof_points: Python, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy async, PostgreSQL, pgvector, Redis, Docker, Linux, REST APIs and event-driven workflows.
React, Vue, Angular, Flutter, JavaScript design systems, WebGL, Three.js, p5 and Canvas.
USD(z), Unity/Unreal workflows, computer vision, 3D mathematics and spatial interfaces.
WCAG and European Accessibility Act implementation experience.

records.title: Agentic AI Requires Judgment
records.summary: Agentic AI success depends on judgment, boundaries, and accountability.
records.body:
Agentic AI is not simply automation.

The challenge is deciding:

- what should be autonomous
- what should remain human
- when escalation is required
- what evidence is sufficient
- who remains accountable

Joz approaches agentic systems as decision systems.

The objective is not maximum autonomy.

The objective is appropriate autonomy supported by trust, governance, observability, and clear ownership.

records.title: Decision Thinking
records.summary: Decision quality matters more than information volume.
records.body:
Joz evaluates systems through the lens of decisions.

Key questions include:

- What decision is being made?
- Who owns it?
- What information supports it?
- How trustworthy is the information?
- What happens if the decision is wrong?
- How quickly can the organization act?

Many organizational problems appear to be technology problems but are actually decision problems.

Improving decision quality often creates disproportionate business value.

records.title: Feedback Loops and Adaptation
records.summary: Healthy systems learn from outcomes and adjust behavior.
records.body:
Systems improve through feedback.

Without feedback loops:

- mistakes repeat
- blind spots grow
- trust declines
- learning slows

Joz looks for mechanisms that connect actions to outcomes.

This principle appears in:

- AI evaluation
- observability
- product analytics
- customer feedback
- governance reviews
- operational metrics

The strongest systems continuously learn from evidence.

records.title: Governance as a System
records.summary: Governance is an enabling mechanism, not merely compliance.
records.body:
Joz views governance as part of system design.

Strong governance creates:

- trust
- accountability
- transparency
- safe autonomy
- sustainable adoption

Weak governance creates uncertainty and resistance.

In AI systems, governance should be embedded into architecture through permissions, observability, evaluation, provenance, and human oversight.

Trust should be designed, not assumed.

records.title: Human Behavior Shapes Systems
records.summary: Systems are ultimately driven by human incentives and behavior.
records.body:
Technology operates inside human systems.

People respond to incentives, trust, workload, goals, and social dynamics.

Joz therefore pays close attention to:

- incentives
- motivation
- ownership
- adoption
- trust
- organizational culture

The most elegant technical solution can fail if human behavior is ignored.

Successful systems align technology with how people actually work.

records.title: Joz's Systems Mindset
records.summary: Organizations are living systems of people, incentives, decisions, workflows, data, and feedback loops.
records.body:
Joz approaches organizations as interconnected systems rather than collections of isolated departments, tools, or projects.

Technology is rarely the root problem.

The underlying challenges are usually:

- poor information flow
- weak decision quality
- unclear ownership
- conflicting incentives
- fragmented knowledge
- missing feedback loops
- low trust
- execution friction

His systems mindset combines enterprise transformation, human-centered design, governance, decision intelligence, and Agentic AI.

The objective is not simply to optimize a process.

The objective is to improve how the entire system learns, adapts, coordinates, and makes decisions over time.

records.title: Organizational Failure Patterns
records.summary: Many failures repeat across industries regardless of technology.
records.body:
Common organizational failure patterns include:

- dashboard overload
- AI pilot fatigue
- duplicated work
- unclear ownership
- information asymmetry
- governance theatre
- excessive approvals
- disconnected incentives

Joz views these as system-level problems.

The solution is rarely another dashboard or another meeting.

The solution is redesigning information flow, accountability, and feedback loops.

records.title: Context Creates Intelligence
records.summary: Data without context rarely creates usable intelligence.
records.body:
Joz believes intelligence emerges from context rather than isolated data.

A metric, document, report, or model output only becomes meaningful when connected to:

- purpose
- history
- ownership
- constraints
- confidence
- business impact

This principle directly influences his work in RAG, context engineering, memory systems, and enterprise knowledge architectures.

Context creates understanding.
Understanding creates better decisions.

records.title: Signal Over Noise
records.summary: More information does not automatically create better decisions.
records.body:
One of Joz's core principles is signal over noise.

Organizations rarely suffer from a lack of information.

They suffer from an inability to identify what matters.

The goal of intelligence systems is not to generate more reports, dashboards, notifications, or alerts.

The goal is to surface the highest-signal information, explain why it matters, and connect it to action.

Good systems reduce cognitive load.
Bad systems increase it.

records.title: Systems Before Features
records.summary: Understand the system before adding technology.
records.body:
Many organizations attempt to solve systemic problems with additional features or tools.

Joz prefers to understand:

- incentives
- workflows
- ownership
- bottlenecks
- feedback loops
- governance

before introducing technology.

Adding AI to a broken system often amplifies existing problems.

Improving the system first usually creates more sustainable value.
